description: Gardenia (Gardenia jasminoides) is a popular fragrant flowering shrub that is mildly toxic to cats. It contains iridoid glycosides — primarily genipin and gardenoside — found throughout the plant, including the flowers and fruit. These compounds irritate the gastrointestinal tract and may trigger skin reactions on contact.
name: Gardenia
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: iridoid glycosides (genipin, gardenoside) in leaves, fruit, and flowers
Mechanism: genipin acts as a GI irritant and may cause mild liver stress at higher doses
Symptoms: vomiting, diarrhea, hives or skin rash (urticaria) from contact; mild lethargy
Symptoms are generally mild and resolve without treatment, but seek vet advice if vomiting is severe or prolonged
The fragrant flowers are attractive to some cats — keep the plant out of easy reach
